q: An empty head is not really empty; it is stuffed with rubbish. Hence the difficulty of forcing anything into an empty head.
h:
“An empty head is not really empty; it is stuffed with rubbish. Hence the difficulty of forcing anything into an empty head.” —
Eric Hoffer

q: Nobody goes through life without a scar.
h:
“Nobody goes through life without a scar.” —
Carol Burnett

q: Strategy is a commodity, execution is an art.
h:
“Strategy is a commodity, execution is an art.” —
Peter Drucker

q: There is no failure except in no longer trying. There is no defeat except from within, no insurmountable barrier except our own inherent weakness of purpose.
h:
“There is no failure except in no longer trying. There is no defeat except from within, no insurmountable barrier except our own inherent weakness of purpose.” —
Elbert Hubbard

q: Just throw away all thoughts of imaginary things, and stand firm in that which you are.
h:
“Just throw away all thoughts of imaginary things, and stand firm in that which you are.” —
Kabir

q: Go for it now. The future is promised to no one.
h:
“Go for it now. The future is promised to no one.” —
Wayne Dyer

q: Dreams provide nourishment for the soul, just as a meal does for the body.
h:
“Dreams provide nourishment for the soul, just as a meal does for the body.” —
Paulo Coelho

q: Time you enjoy wasting, was not wasted.
h:
“Time you enjoy wasting, was not wasted.” —
John Lennon

q: Blessed is he who expects nothing, for he shall be disappointed.
h:
“Blessed is he who expects nothing, for he shall be disappointed.” —
Jonathan Swift

q: My sun sets to rise again.
h:
“My sun sets to rise again.” —
Robert Browning

q: The wise man reads both books and life itself.
h:
“The wise man reads both books and life itself.” —
Lin Yutang

q: Desire is suffering.
h:
“Desire is suffering.” —
Naval Ravikant

q: Confidence is what you have before you understand the problem.
h:
“Confidence is what you have before you understand the problem.” —
Woody Allen

q: Hardly anybody recognizes the most significant moments of their life at the time they happen.
h:
“Hardly anybody recognizes the most significant moments of their life at the time they happen.” —
W.P. Kinsella

q: Everyone you admire was once a beginner.
h:
“Everyone you admire was once a beginner.” —
Jack Butcher

q: The only problem we really have is we think we're not supposed to have problems! Problems call us to higher level- - face & solve them now!
h:
“The only problem we really have is we think we're not supposed to have problems! Problems call us to higher level- - face & solve them now!” —
Tony Robbins

q: Honesty is a very expensive gift, Don't expect it from cheap people.
h:
“Honesty is a very expensive gift, Don't expect it from cheap people.” —
Warren Buffett

q: Start each day with a positive thought and a grateful heart.
h:
“Start each day with a positive thought and a grateful heart.” —
Roy T. Bennett

q: Blessings and burdens are not mutually exclusive.
h:
“Blessings and burdens are not mutually exclusive.” —
Ryan Holiday

q: Your world is a living expression of how you are using and have used your mind.
h:
“Your world is a living expression of how you are using and have used your mind.” —
Earl Nightingale

q: The worst part of success is trying to find someone who is happy for you.
h:
“The worst part of success is trying to find someone who is happy for you.” —
Bette Midler

q: Why fit in when you were born to stand out?
h:
“Why fit in when you were born to stand out?” —
Dr. Seuss

q: A single act of kindness throws out roots in all directions, and the roots spring up and make new trees.
h:
“A single act of kindness throws out roots in all directions, and the roots spring up and make new trees.” —
Amelia Earhart

q: It is best to begin at the beginning.
h:
“It is best to begin at the beginning.” —
Amelia Earhart

q: Try not to become a man of success, but rather try to become a man of value.
h:
“Try not to become a man of success, but rather try to become a man of value.” —
Albert Einstein

q: Optimism is the faith that leads to achievement. Nothing can be done without hope and confidence.
h:
“Optimism is the faith that leads to achievement. Nothing can be done without hope and confidence.” —
Helen Keller

q: My favorite things in life don't cost any money. It's really clear that the most precious resource we all have is time.
h:
“My favorite things in life don't cost any money. It's really clear that the most precious resource we all have is time.” —
Steve Jobs

q: To be alive - is Power.
h:
“To be alive - is Power.” —
Emily Dickinson

q: Natural talent only determines the limits of your athletic potential. It's dedication and a willingness to discipline your life that makes you great.
h:
“Natural talent only determines the limits of your athletic potential. It's dedication and a willingness to discipline your life that makes you great.” —
Billie Jean King

q: All problems are illusions of the mind.
h:
“All problems are illusions of the mind.” —
Eckhart Tolle

q: As a man thinketh in his heart, so shall he be.
h:
“As a man thinketh in his heart, so shall he be.” —
James Allen

q: Getting over a painful experience is much like crossing monkey bars. You have to let go at some point in order to move forward.
h:
“Getting over a painful experience is much like crossing monkey bars. You have to let go at some point in order to move forward.” —
Unknown

q: Don't let the noise of others opinions drown out your inner voice.
h:
“Don't let the noise of others opinions drown out your inner voice.” —
Steve Jobs

q: Never waste a minute of your precious life thinking about people you don't like.
h:
“Never waste a minute of your precious life thinking about people you don't like.” —
Celestine Chua

q: The quality of your life is the quality of your relationships.
h:
“The quality of your life is the quality of your relationships.” —
Tony Robbins

q: All men are equal before fish.
h:
“All men are equal before fish.” —
Herbert Hoover

q: It is dangerous to be right in matters on which the established authorities are wrong.
h:
“It is dangerous to be right in matters on which the established authorities are wrong.” —
Voltaire

q: When awareness arises in you, it means awareness is arising in the collective mind of humanity.
h:
“When awareness arises in you, it means awareness is arising in the collective mind of humanity.” —
Eckhart Tolle

q: You can calculate the worth of a man by the number of his enemies.
h:
“You can calculate the worth of a man by the number of his enemies.” —
Gustave Flaubert

q: Children are our greatest natural resource.
h:
“Children are our greatest natural resource.” —
Herbert Hoover

q: You are free, and that is why you are lost.
h:
“You are free, and that is why you are lost.” —
Franz Kafka

q: Life begins at the end of our comfort zone.
h:
“Life begins at the end of our comfort zone.” —
Neale Donald Walsch

q: Genius is patience.
h:
“Genius is patience.” —
Isaac Newton

q: Whatever happened, happened for the good. Whatever is happening, is happening for the good. Whatever will happen, will also happen for the good.
h:
“Whatever happened, happened for the good. Whatever is happening, is happening for the good. Whatever will happen, will also happen for the good.” —
Bhagavad Gita

q: No man is happy unless he believes he is.
h:
“No man is happy unless he believes he is.” —
Publilius Syrus

q: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
h:
“We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.” —
Aristotle

q: Always try to be a little kinder than necessary.
h:
“Always try to be a little kinder than necessary.” —
James Matthew Barrie

q: Not how long, but how well you have lived is the main thing.
h:
“Not how long, but how well you have lived is the main thing.” —
Seneca

q: The four P's to success are pressure, persistence, perseverance, and prayer.
h:
“The four P's to success are pressure, persistence, perseverance, and prayer.” —
Steve Harvey

q: The Law of Concentration states that whatever you dwell upon grows. The more you think about something, the more it becomes part of your reality.
h:
“The Law of Concentration states that whatever you dwell upon grows. The more you think about something, the more it becomes part of your reality.” —
Brian Tracy
